children.label: A
children.text: the governing board shall be composed of members who broadly represent or are knowledgeable about the needs of the individuals served by the system;

children.label: i
children.text: individuals with disabilities, including individuals with developmental disabilities, who are eligible for services, or have received or are receiving services through the system; or

children.label: ii
children.text: parents, family members, guardians, advocates, or authorized representatives of individuals referred to in clause (i); and

children.label: C
children.text: the board may include a representative of the State Council on Developmental Disabilities, the Centers in the State, and the self-advocacy organization described in ;section 15024(c)(4)(A)(ii)(I) of this title

not more than ⅓ of the members of the governing board may be appointed by the chief executive officer of the State involved, in the case of any State in which such officer has the authority to appoint members of the board;
the membership of the governing board shall be subject to term limits set by the system to ensure rotating membership;
any vacancy in the board shall be filled not later than 60 days after the date on which the vacancy occurs; and

children.label: A
children.text: that shall advise the system on policies and priorities to be carried out in protecting and advocating the rights of individuals with developmental disabilities; and

children.label: i
children.text: individuals with developmental disabilities who are eligible for services, or have received or are receiving services, through the system; or

children.label: ii
children.text: parents, family members, guardians, advocates, or authorized representatives of individuals referred to in clause (i).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter shall preclude a system from bringing a suit on behalf of individuals with developmental disabilities against a State, or an agency or instrumentality of a State.

children.heading: Use of amounts from judgment
children.label: 2 Use of amounts from judgment
children.text: An amount received pursuant to a suit described in paragraph (1) through a court judgment may only be used by the system to further the purpose of this part and shall not be used to augment payments to legal contractors or to award personal bonuses.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 3 Limitation
children.text: The system shall use assistance provided under this part in a manner consistent with .section 14404 of this title

children.heading: Disclosure of information
children.label: c Disclosure of information
children.text: For purposes of any periodic audit, report, or evaluation required under this part, the Secretary shall not require an entity carrying out a program to disclose the identity of, or any other personally identifiable information related to, any individual requesting assistance under such program.

children.heading: Public notice of Federal onsite review
children.label: d Public notice of Federal onsite review
children.text: The Secretary shall provide advance public notice of any Federal programmatic or administrative onsite review of a system conducted under this part and solicit public comment on the system through such notice. The Secretary shall prepare an onsite visit report containing the results of such review, which shall be distributed to the Governor of the State and to other interested public and private parties. The comments received in response to the public comment solicitation notice shall be included in the onsite visit report.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: e Reports
children.text: Beginning in fiscal year 2002, each system established in a State pursuant to this part shall annually prepare and transmit to the Secretary a report that describes the activities, accomplishments, and expenditures of the system during the preceding fiscal year, including a description of the system’s goals, the extent to which the goals were achieved, barriers to their achievement, the process used to obtain public input, the nature of such input, and how such input was used.
